description.value:
A carved ivory figurine of a fox, its expression caught mid-smirk. The craftsmanship is excellent - fine detail, clean lines, clearly the work of a skilled artisan. It has been valued at exactly 60 gp by three separate appraisers, which is slightly unusual because normally they disagree.

Curse. Anyone who owns this figurine is observed. Not stalked, not tracked - simply watched. Twice per week, the GM chooses an NPC who "happens" to see the party in an unexpected location and remembers them clearly. These sightings are not always threatening, but word travels. Information about the party's whereabouts, appearance, and activities accumulates in the world in ways that eventually become inconvenient.

unidentified.description: A carved ivory fox figurine. Excellent craftsmanship. Appraised at 60 gp. The fox looks rather pleased with itself.

cursedMeta.decoyAppearance: An ivory fox figurine. Excellent craft. Worth 60 gp.
cursedMeta.trueNature: Owner is observed: 2x per week an NPC witnesses the party unexpectedly and remembers them. Information accumulates.

curse.name: The Twice-Seen
curse.description: While the figurine is owned, interested parties learn about the group twice per week through credible NPC sightings that spread gossip and accurate detail.

hints: Strangers greet the owner as if they already saw them somewhere implausible.

escalation.effect: Odd coincidences: the same face in two different crowds the same day.
